message:
feat(frankenpandas): round out SQL reader prelude surface (fd90.244 / br-7rtan)

The prelude already had read_sql / read_sql_chunks / read_sql_table /
read_sql_with_options. Four more user-facing SQL readers were exposed
at top-level only:

- read_sql_query — standalone query form (no table-name lookup)
- read_sql_query_with_options
- read_sql_table_chunks — table-based chunked iteration
- read_sql_table_with_options

Fix:
- Add the 4 variants to the prelude
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard with name-checks

The *_with_index_col + *_with_options_and_index_col specialized
variants are still top-level-only (less common, not a parity claim
in the README's IO Format Support table).

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-7rg5","title":"Add series str.swapcase unicode edge strict conformance packets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T02:20:17.218902181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T02:27:51.269661559Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T02:27:51.267891155Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-7rtan","title":"fd90.244: Add read_sql_query + read_sql_table_chunks family to prelude","description":"fd90.244: Add read_sql_query / read_sql_table_chunks variants to the prelude.

The prelude has read_sql/read_sql_chunks/read_sql_table/read_sql_with_options. Several other user-facing SQL readers exposed at top-level are missing from prelude:

- read_sql_query (standalone query form — no table-name lookup)
- read_sql_query_with_options
- read_sql_query_chunks
- read_sql_query_chunks_with_options
- read_sql_table_chunks (table-based chunked iteration)
- read_sql_table_chunks_with_options
- read_sql_table_with_options

Scope (keep tight):
- Add the 4 most user-relevant variants to the prelude:
  read_sql_query, read_sql_query_with_options, read_sql_table_chunks, read_sql_table_with_options
- Skip the *_with_index_col + *_with_options_and_index_col variants — they're more specialized
